name: Barack Obama
executiveSummary:
Barack Obama is an American politician and retired attorney who served as the 44th president of the United States from 2009 to 2017. He was the first African American president in U.S. history.

During his presidency, he enacted landmark legislation including the Affordable Care Act, the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act, and ordered the military operation that resulted in the death of Osama bin Laden. Post-presidency, he focuses on the Obama Foundation, writing bestselling memoirs, and producing content via Higher Ground Productions.
biography:
Obama was born in Honolulu, Hawaii. After graduating from Columbia University in 1983, he worked as a community organizer in Chicago. In 1988, he enrolled in Harvard Law School, where he was the first black president of the Harvard Law Review. After graduating, he became a civil rights attorney and an academic, teaching constitutional law at the University of Chicago Law School.

He served three terms representing the 13th district in the Illinois Senate from 1997 to 2004, when he ran for the U.S. Senate. He gained national attention for his keynote address at the 2004 Democratic National Convention and won his Senate election in a landslide. In 2008, he defeated Republican nominee John McCain to become president and was re-elected in 2012 against Mitt Romney.

Since leaving office, Obama has remained highly influential in American politics. He and his wife, Michelle, founded Higher Ground Productions, winning an Academy Award for their documentary 'American Factory'. He published his presidential memoir, 'A Promised Land', in 2020.

education.degree: Bachelor of Arts in Political Science

recentNews.title: Campaigning for Democrats
recentNews.description: Active in 2024 political campaigning and fundraising for Democratic candidates.

recentNews.title: Higher Ground Productions
recentNews.description: Continues to produce award-winning films and series for Netflix.
